channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Por que Valdemar perdeu a viagem ao tratar de eleição com Eduardo nos EUA
item.description: Por que Valdemar perdeu a viagem ao tratar de eleição com Eduardo nos EUA

item.title: Atualizar o celular deixa ele mais lento? Entenda o que realmente acontece depois de instalar uma nova versão
item.description: The article addresses the common concern about whether updating a phone makes it slower. It explains that on older devices, new operating system versions may cause slowness or increased battery consumption due to additional features and optimizations designed for newer hardware.

item.title: Coffee Break: PF cumpre 4ª fase de operação contra fraudes na Educação que investiga ex-nora de Lula
item.description: Federal Police launched the fourth phase of Operation Coffee Break, investigating fraud in Education tenders in São Paulo municipalities between 2021 and 2025. Carla Ariane Trindade, Lula's former daughter-in-law, was targeted in the first phase, suspected of receiving kickbacks. Suspects may face charges carrying up to 60 years in prison.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro e María Corina Machado se encontram no Chile e pedem 'justiça' pelo Brasil e Venezuela
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro met with Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado in Chile during the inauguration of President José Antonio Kast. Flávio congratulated Machado on her Nobel Peace Prize and both called for 'justice' in their countries. Meanwhile, President Lula did not attend the ceremony, being represented by Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira.

item.title: Presidente do TSE defende valorização da educação e da justiça ante os desafios da democracia
item.description: The president of the TSE, Minister Cármen Lúcia, defended the importance of legal education and academic deepening to face contemporary challenges in Law, democracy, and labor relations. She highlighted that the world is experiencing profound changes, especially in social and labor relations, driven by technological transformation. The minister emphasized that human dignity remains the main pillar for interpreting new social and labor relations.

item.title: Para o governo, aposta em Bolsonaro levou Raízen à crise bilionária
item.description: Minister Alexandre Silveira blamed Raízen's billion-dollar crisis on political bets made by its shareholders during the 2022 election, including 5 billion reais in Vale investments and 4 billion reais in fuel tax cuts, based on expectations of Bolsonaro's re-election. Despite the problems, the government plans to rescue the company, deemed strategic for the country.

item.title: Atraso em reunião entre Lula e Trump abriu espaço para ala ideológica no governo dos EUA, dizem diplomatas
item.description: The delay in the meeting between Lula and Trump reduced direct dialogue space and strengthened the ideological wing of the US government. Diplomats assess that the delay cooled the relationship between the presidents, allowing figures like Marco Rubio and Darren Beattie to gain influence on policies related to Brazil. There is concern in the Brazilian government about possible US interference in national sovereignty issues.

item.title: 7 pontos sobre a nova pesquisa Quaest, que mostra empate entre Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro no 2º turno
item.description: Quaest poll shows unprecedented tie between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in 2nd round scenario, both with 41% voting intentions. The survey indicates worsening government evaluation and economic perception, reinforcing polarization scenario six months before the election.

item.title: Genial/Quaest: Maioria vê STF com poder demais e considera votar em candidato pró-impeachment
item.description: Genial/Quaest poll shows 72% of Brazilians believe the STF has too much power and 66% consider it important to vote for candidates favoring the impeachment of ministers. Distrust in the Supreme Court has increased, with 49% saying they don't trust the institution versus 43% who do.

item.title: Casa que sediou campanha de Lula em 2022 será QG de Haddad em SP
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad will use the same Pacaembu house that served as Lula's campaign base in 2022 for his run for São Paulo governor in 2026. Haddad has already defined part of his campaign team, including his marketer and coordinators, and is expected to leave the ministry next week.

item.title: Paulinho da Força aposta em perfil desvinculado de Lula e Bolsonaro para se eleger ao Senado
item.description: Federal deputy Paulinho da Força, a Senate candidate for São Paulo, is betting on his image being unlinked to Lula and Bolsonaro to get elected. He believes his political neutrality could be an advantage in the electoral race.

item.title: China endurece su política hacia las minorías con una nueva ley para impulsar la “unidad étnica”
item.description: China is implementing a new law to strengthen ethnic unity, with ethnic minorities displaying their traditional attire at a major political meeting in Beijing.

item.title: Notícias - Partido Novo
item.description: Minas Gerais Governor and NOVO presidential pre-candidate Romeu Zema filed an impeachment request against STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes in a press conference. The event, attended by dozens of party leaders, was motivated by revelations about messages between Moraes and banker Daniel Vorcaro in the Master Case.

item.title: Campanha de Flávio dá ultimato para Ratinho Jr desistir de candidatura à Presidência
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Jr. met with Rogério Marinho and was asked to drop his presidential bid in favor of Flávio Bolsonaro. Ratinho Jr. rejected the proposal to support Flávio and be considered for vice president.

item.title: Governo Lula vê nova onda de influência de apoiadores de Bolsonaro na gestão Trump para classificar facções como terroristas
item.description: The Lula government detected influence from Bolsonaro's allies in White House decisions to classify Brazilian criminal factions as terrorists. This classification could open the door for US military interventions in Brazil and harm national sovereignty. The Itamaraty views this as an alliance between Trumpist radicals and Brazilian right-wing to create a trap for the Brazilian government in an election year.

item.title: Tarcísio diz que EUA declararem PCC como terrorista é “oportunidade”
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas called the potential US designation of PCC and CV as terrorist organizations an 'opportunity,' saying it would facilitate international cooperation in fighting crime. This position contrasts with the federal government's stance that criminal factions cannot be considered terrorists as they do not defend ideological causes.

item.title: José Manuel Restrepo: “Si buscan votos, se equivocaron de candidato”
item.description: José Manuel Restrepo, former Finance Minister and rector of EIA University, accepted to be Abelardo de la Espriella's vice-presidential candidate, despite initially denying political ambitions. The swift change of position reveals the accelerated and surprising nature of the electoral process.

item.title: Centrão mapeia votos no STF para tentar soltar Daniel Vorcaro e evitar delação
item.description: The Centrão is mapping votes in the STF to try to free banker Daniel Vorcaro and prevent him from making a plea bargain. With Toffoli's recusal, the Second Panel would judge the case with four ministers, and a possible tie would favor the defendant, meaning Vorcaro's freedom.

item.title: Durante seu programa, Ratinho faz comentário transfóbico contra deputada Érika Hilton
item.description: TV host Ratinho made transphobic comments on his program criticizing the election of Congresswoman Erika Hilton as president of the Women's Commission. He claimed that 'to be a woman you have to be a woman' and questioned Hilton's gender identity, suggesting she is not a 'real woman' because she doesn't have a uterus or menstruate.

item.title: Datafolha: Descrédito de instituições cresce, e 52% desconfiam de partidos políticos
item.description: Datafolha survey reveals growing distrust in Brazilian institutions between December 2024 and March 2026, with confidence or public evaluation worsening for 7 out of 8 surveyed institutions. The scenario shows widespread skepticism, especially with 52% of the population distrusting political parties.

item.title: TSE julga caso que pode criar jurisprudência sobre desvio de verbas de cotas raciais na eleição
item.description: The TSE will judge this Thursday a case that may create jurisprudence on the diversion of electoral fund quotas intended for black candidacies, which are diverted through donations to white candidates.

item.title: Avanço de Flávio nas pesquisas faz Lula acelerar decisões sobre palanques em SP e MG
item.description: Polls show Flávio Bolsonaro advancing in the second round, worrying the presidential palace. Lula accelerates decisions on electoral alliances in SP and MG, seeking to close tickets with Haddad and Pacheco to contain the growth of Bolsonarism.

item.title: Marina é cotada para palanque de Lula em SP enquanto eleições e soja pressionam desmatamento
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva is set to leave her post to join President Lula's campaign in São Paulo. Her departure comes amid concerns that deforestation may rise due to the election year and the suspension of the soy moratorium.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro agradece pessoalmente a Milei por asilo a condenado do 8 de Janeiro
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro personally thanked Argentine President Milei for granting political asylum to a Brazilian convicted for the January 8th attacks. The case involves Joel Borges Corrêa, who received refuge in Argentina after being sentenced to 13 years and 6 months in prison. Milei responded by saying Bolsonaro 'has already won' the 2026 presidential elections.

item.title: Câmara aprova criação de política nacional de assistência jurídica às vítimas de violência
item.description: The Chamber of Deputies approved the creation of the National Policy for Legal Assistance to Victims of Violence (Pnajov), which provides mandatory legal support to vulnerable groups including women, children, elderly, and people with disabilities. The bill ensures free assistance through public defenders, Public Prosecutors, and pro bono lawyers from the Brazilian Bar Association, while preventing institutional revictimization and guaranteeing victims' active participation in legal proceedings.

item.title: Dia do Bibliotecário: TSE disponibiliza versão atualizada do Tesauro da Justiça Eleitoral
item.description: The TSE releases the 8th edition of the Electoral Justice Thesaurus, a tool that standardizes specialized terms in Electoral Law. The publication features 4,577 terms and 314 scope notes, facilitating research and document organization. The tool is intended for servers, researchers, libraries, and those interested in the Brazilian electoral process.

item.title: Enviado de Trump busca agenda com Bolsonaro sem ter pedido reunião com Planalto ou Itamaraty
item.description: Special envoy of Trump's government to Brazil, Darren Beattie, did not request official meetings with Itamaraty or Planalto, but sought a meeting with Jair Bolsonaro after the former president's defense requested it.

item.title: Vídeo: Senado aprova: informações de saúde para trabalhadores e Estatuto do Paciente
item.description: The Senate approved the requirement for companies to inform workers about their right to take leave for preventive exams for HPV, cervical cancer, and prostate cancer. The Patient's Rights Statute was also approved and will now go to the President for signing.

item.title: Toffoli se declara suspeito e não votará em referendo sobre a prisão de Daniel Vorcaro
item.description: Minister Dias Toffoli declared himself biased to judge the banker Daniel Vorcaro's arrest, abstaining from voting in the referendum on Minister André Mendonça's decision. Toffoli cited personal relationships with Vorcaro's group as the reason for his bias, having previously relinquished the investigation's leadership. The case will now be judged by Ministers Gilmar Mendes, Kássio Nunes Marques, Luiz Fux, and rapporteur André Mendonça.

item.title: Relator da PEC da escala 6×1 defende período de transição e confirma votação em abril
item.description: Deputy Paulo Azi confirmed that voting on the 6x1 scale PEC in the Chamber's CCJ will take place in April, with possible approval in the plenary before the parliamentary recess in July. He advocates a transition period and points to two main divergences: whether the scale reduction should be in the Constitution and the economic repercussions for different sectors.

item.title: Presidente da CPI do INSS faz apelo a Mendonça para que comissão possa interrogar Vorcaro
item.description: The president of the INSS CPI, Carlos Viana, appealed to STF Minister André Mendonça to allow the commission to question Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master. The request comes in response to habeas corpus that prevent the involved parties from appearing before Congress.

item.title: Medalha JK homenageia senadores por contribuição ao setor de transportes
item.description: Four senators received the JK Medal from the National Transportation Confederation for contributions to Brazil's transportation and logistics sector. The highest honor, the Grand Cross, was awarded to Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who emphasized the importance of national integration through infrastructure investments. Senators Marcos Rogério, Laércio Oliveira, and Veneziano Vital do Rêgo were also honored, highlighting the importance of collaboration between public and private sectors.

item.title: Presidente da CPI do INSS diz que convocação de Vorcaro será votada na Segunda Turma do STF
item.description: President of the INSS CPI, Senator Carlos Viana, says STF Minister André Mendonça will submit the Vorcaro case to the Second Panel. Viana criticizes judicial interference in CPI investigations and emphasizes that bank presidents will be heard as witnesses, not as investigated parties.

item.title: Áudio: Senadores autorizam monitoramento de agressores por IA
item.description: The Senate approved a bill allowing AI monitoring of aggressors under Maria da Penha protective measures, aiming to protect victims and implement state prevention actions. The text now goes to the Chamber of Deputies for analysis.

item.title: Áudio: Senado ganha sala de acolhimento para mulheres vítimas de violência
item.description: The Senate inaugurated the Sala Lílás, the first support room for women victims of violence in a Parliament worldwide. The initiative is part of the Antes que Aconteça program, created by Senator Daniella Ribeiro, aimed at preventing gender violence. The project may become law after Senate approval.

item.title: Áudio: Roberta Acioly assume vaga de Mecias de Jesus e vai representar Roraima no Senado
item.description: Roberta Acioly took office as senator for Roraima, replacing Mecias de Jesus, who will become a member of the State Audit Court. With a background in health, including nursing, dentistry, and a master's in collective health, she will hold the position until January 31, 2027.

item.title: Justiça aceita pedido de recuperação extrajudicial do Pão de Açúcar
item.description: GPA has obtained judicial approval for its out-of-court restructuring, enabling it to renegotiate R$ 4.5 billion in debts with creditors. The plan only affects unsecured debts while preserving payments to workers, suppliers, and partners.

item.title: Com renda declarada de R$ 570 milhões em 2024, Vorcaro recebeu restituição do Imposto de Renda
item.description: Owner of Banco Master, Daniel Vorcaro, declared R$ 570 million in income for 2024 and received a tax refund of R$ 28,764.97. His wealth nearly doubled in a year marked by cash problems at the bank and investigations. Most income came from sales of company shares, with capital gains generating R$ 124 million in taxes.

item.title: Culpa não é minha se Haddad aumentou imposto a cada 30 dias, diz Tarcísio sobre rival para eleição
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas criticized Finance Minister Fernando Haddad, claiming he increased taxes every 30 days during his tenure. Tarcísio, likely running for re-election, directed attacks at his potential opponent in the state election.

item.title: 🚨 URGENTE: Flávio Bolsonaro aparece à frente de Lula nas pesquisas! eleitoral
item.description: Sensationalist title about poll allegedly showing Flávio Bolsonaro leading Lula. Content only shows video playback error without concrete information.

item.title: As dificuldades de Lula para se reeleger não são apenas momentâneas
item.description: As dificuldades de Lula para se reeleger não são apenas momentâneas

item.title: Senado aprova uso de IA para proteger vítimas de violência doméstica
item.description: The Senate approved a bill using AI to protect domestic violence victims, including real-time monitoring of aggressors and an emergency app. The program provides electronic ankle bracelets and wearable devices to detect aggressor proximity and send immediate alerts to authorities.

item.title: Adiado depoimento de Leila Pereira na CPMI do INSS
item.description: Leila Pereira's testimony at the INSS CPMI was postponed to March 18th after Minister Flávio Dino's decision to exempt her from appearing due to an international commitment. The CPMI president criticized the decision, questioning the precedence of personal agendas over the duty to clarify matters to Congress.

item.title: Sessão especial vai celebrar o aniversário de Brasília
item.description: The Senate will hold special sessions to celebrate Brasília's 66th anniversary and Marília University's 70th anniversary. An applause vote was also approved for the film 'O Agente Secreto' which won at the Golden Globe.

item.title: A 1ª grande derrota legislativa de Sheinbaum no México
item.description: Claudia Sheinbaum suffered her first major legislative defeat in Mexico when deputies rejected her electoral reform proposal that sought to cut party funding by 25% and reduce public-funded electoral propaganda time. The initiative required a two-thirds majority but received 259 votes in favor against 234 opposed, falling short among the 494 deputies present. The reform also aimed to eliminate proportional lists for choosing legislators, a measure criticized by allies and analysts who claimed it would harm small parties.

item.title: Justiça Eleitoral pelo Brasil: Museu Eleitoral de João Pessoa preserva memória da JE paraibana
item.description: The Electoral Court of Paraíba inaugurated an electoral museum in João Pessoa, preserving the history of the Electoral Justice in the state. The space displays original furniture from the Court's first headquarters and old ballot boxes, showing the evolution of the democratic process in Paraíba.

item.title: Após Toffoli se declarar suspeito, Zanin é sorteado como relator de ação sobre CPI do Master
item.description: Minister Cristiano Zanin was drawn as the new rapporteur at the STF for an action regarding the Master CPI, after Dias Toffoli declared himself suspect due to conflicts of interest. The action requests the installation of a CPI to investigate alleged frauds in the purchase of Banco Master by Banco de Brasília, with support from 200 deputies.

item.title: Mpox não é crise nacional, mas exige reforço na vacinação de grupos prioritários
item.description: Brazil reports 140 confirmed Mpox cases in 2026, mainly concentrated in São Paulo, with no deaths. Experts say there is no national crisis but warn about the need to reinforce vaccination in priority groups, including people living with HIV and laboratory professionals.

item.title: Wagner Moura será um dos apresentadores do Oscar
item.description: Brazilian actor Wagner Moura has been announced as an Oscar 2026 presenter, where he is nominated for Best Actor. The ceremony takes place next Sunday with other celebrities including Pedro Pascal, Nicole Kidman, and Ewan McGregor.

item.title: Em palestra, ministra Cármen Lúcia classifica feminicídios no Brasil como “barbárie”
item.description: Minister Cármen Lúcia classified feminicides in Brazil as 'barbarism' and called for an immediate end to violence against women. In a lecture at TCU, she highlighted that the increased presence of women in public spaces has provoked violent reactions and emphasized the need to transform society into one of equals.
